extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won Stage 2 sprint by a quarter-wheel over JAKOBSEN Fabio on a wide boulevard after a mistimed lead-out from Deceuninck
tactical_patterns: PHILIPSEN won from far back left side while JAKOBSEN led out too early on wide boulevard, showing positioning beats pure speed
Deceuninck hit front too early with 1.5km dead straight finish, allowing swarm on wide open road
DEMARE Arnold finished 14th, nowhere near top-5, revealing chronic positioning weakness consistent with Tour de France performance

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Third consecutive opening sprint stage win for Alpecin across 2021 Grand Tours, demonstrating exceptional early-race form pattern
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: Led too early despite strong acceleration, suggesting team timing issues on ultra-wide finishes
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnold: negative: 14th place repeat of Tour positioning failures, not even in picture on perfect big-man boulevard finish
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Stayed near front avoiding danger, maintained GC lead without incident
